memory.summary: The scholarship deadline is approaching with a manageable workload, but the immediate fun option offers an easy escape from my current stress and sleep debt.

Starting this scholarship application early is a low-stress optimization that absorbs potential friction without sacrificing my planned fun later. I can submit the form confidently now to reduce Thursday's pressure.
I have only completed a small fraction of the scholarship work within my free block, leaving the majority untouched as time runs out.
I advanced the scholarship application but missed a low-stress fun opportunity, leaving me with moderate stress and sleep debt as the deadline approaches.
I advanced the scholarship application by 8%, but I am still behind schedule with moderate stress and sleep debt accumulating as the deadline nears.
I've completed 66% of the scholarship application in 1.5 hours, but I still have two focused hours left before the deadline with rising stress and sleep debt.
I made solid progress on the scholarship essay, but my energy is critically low and sleep debt remains unaddressed as the deadline approaches.
I've advanced the scholarship application to 78%, but my critically low energy and sleep debt make continuing ineffective right now.
I've reached 84% on the scholarship application, but my critically low energy and sleep debt make continuing productive right now.
I made minimal progress on the scholarship application despite working late, confirming that my critically low energy and sleep debt are severely hindering productivity.
I reached 95% on the scholarship application, but my zero energy and high stress confirm that working further is counterproductive.
The scholarship application is now complete, but my zero energy and high sleep debt mean I must stop working immediately to recover.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: The scholarship deadline is in 3 days with only 5% progress; delaying risks burnout later. While fun_now offers immediate relief, skipping it now increases stress (4) and jeopardizes the high-priority goal. Working 2 hours advances the application significantly without exhausting remaining energy.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
